Ad Text:
You will play a key role in a major digital transformation project already underway, responsible for ensuring it delivers against our business requirements and built to support where the business is heading next. The team includes a part-time Technical Project Manager who owns delivery coordination, while you will be responsible for the 'how'-mapping our processes, identifying automation opportunities using cutting-edge AI, and ensuring our staff and contractors are expertly trained.

You won't be operating in isolation - but you will be expected to bring strong technical and process judgement, make decisions confidently, and communicate clearly at a senior level.



Key Responsibilities:

Process Mapping & Deep Discovery

Conduct deep-dive discovery sessions to map 'As-Is' processes across clinical, administrative, and growth teams.
Develop clear, visual 'To-Be' process maps that integrate new system capabilities and automation.
Analyse operational data to identify high-impact automation projects that reduce manual effort and human error.

Automation & AI Implementation

Design and build automated workflows using AI tools (LLMs, Claude etc.) and integration platforms (Zapier/Make) to streamline the business.
Stay on the absolute cutting edge of AI developments, proactively implementing new tools that give Dokotela a competitive advantage.

Knowledge Management & Training

Own and maintain the Dokotela Knowledge Base, ensuring it is the single source of truth for all operational processes.
Develop and lead training programs for staff and contractors, ensuring high adoption of new systems and automated workflows.

Quality Assurance (QA) Frameworks

Design robust QA processes and checklists for Team Leads to implement, ensuring quality remains high as we automate.
Monitor process health through data, iterating on solutions to ensure they meet clinical and business standards.

Qualifications and Skills

Essential

4+ years of experience in process improvement, digital transformation, or operations management.
Proven ability to map complex processes and translate them into simplified, automated workflows.
Advanced proficiency with AI productivity tools and a deep curiosity for emerging tech.
Experience in Knowledge Management-building and maintaining internal 'Source of Truth' documentation.
Strong training and communication skills; able to influence and educate both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Desirable

Experience in Healthcare.

Ad Text:
Are you keen to design and develop world-class capabilities, turning data into knowledge and operational outcomes? Are you passionate about building solutions to allow data to tell its story? ASIS offers an opportunity to achieve these goals in an uncompromisingly unique environment. As a Data Professional in ASIS, you will have the chance to actively enable our operational teams to make the best use of ASIS’s unique data assets.

ASIS leverages the wide world of open-source software and supplements it with our own development to deliver the bespoke tools required for the ASIS mission. Among our core technologies are Python, Scala, Java, TypeScript, and JavaScript. Our stack includes Spring, Vue, PyTorch, Tensorflow, Apache Spark, Accumulo, Hadoop, NiFi, Solr, Ansible and Git. We leverage structured and unstructured data, including geospatial and multilingual datasets. Ideal candidates will be extraordinarily innovative, self-motivated and keen to use these toolsets to craft new systems that are simply not found in any other organisation. If this sounds like an environment you could thrive in, then apply for one of these unique positions.

Specialisations

We have a number of positions available including:

Software Engineers at L4 to EL1
Data Engineers at L 6 to EL1
Data Technicians at L4 to Level 6
System Engineers at L4 to EL1
DevOps Engineers at L4 to EL1
Data Centre Facilities Manager at EL1 to EL2 (Canberra only)
For more detail please visit the ASIS website (Current Vacancies) and review the selection document under the Talent Register - Data Engineering Professionals L4 - EL2

AI Solutions Architect
Design Enterprise Anthropic Integrations 

Galileo Search
Sydney / Contract / Hybrid

Engagement: Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

Enterprise Australia is moving to AI-first operations — but most companies have no idea how to architect the transition. They have legacy ERP systems, on-premise data, compliance requirements, and thousands of employees who've never used an AI tool. Someone needs to design how Claude fits into that reality.  That someone is you.

Galileo Search is building a specialist talent pool of certified Anthropic professionals who deliver enterprise Claude and Cowork deployments.

As a Solutions Architect on the Galileo marketplace, you'll be the person who walks into a complex enterprise environment, understands their stack, and designs how Anthropic's ecosystem integrates with everything they already have.

What you'll do on client engagements
Assess client technology landscapes and design Claude integration architecture — what connects to what, how data flows, where AI adds value
Define security architecture for Claude deployments — data classification, access controls, API key management, audit logging
Design MCP server topology — which internal systems Claude needs to access and how to do it securely at scale
Create architecture decision records, integration patterns, and technical roadmaps that engineering teams can execute against
Present to C-suite and technology leadership — translate technical architecture into business outcomes
Guide Galileo's Integration Engineers and Config Specialists through implementation of your designs
What you bring
8+ years in technology roles with at least 3 years in architecture or technical leadership
Enterprise architecture experience — you've designed systems that serve thousands of users across multiple business units
Cloud platform expertise (AWS, Azure, or GCP) including networking, security, and identity
Understanding of enterprise integration patterns — APIs, event-driven architecture, middleware, ETL
Ability to communicate architecture decisions to both engineers and executives
Experience in regulated industries (financial services, government, healthcare) is highly valued
Bonus if you have the following (but not required)
Prior experience designing AI/ML system architecture
Familiarity with Anthropic's Claude API, MCP protocol, or Cowork platform
TOGAF, AWS Solutions Architect, or equivalent certification
Experience with zero-trust security models
What Galileo offers
Great contract day rates — reflecting the seniority and impact of the role
Free Anthropic certification and training to complement your existing architecture skills
A delivery team underneath you — Integration Engineers, Config Specialists, and PMs who execute your vision
To apply: Share your CV and a brief overview of the most complex integration architecture you've designed. We're looking for people who think in systems, not just components.

Anthropic Integration Engineer
Join Australia's first AI Talent Marketplace
Ongoing client assignments through Galileo Search

Sydney / Contract / Hybrid 

The way companies work is about to fundamentally change. Anthropic's Claude and Cowork are replacing legacy workflows across enterprises right now — and the companies making the move need engineers who can build the integrations that make it happen.

Galileo Search is building Australia's first specialist marketplace of certified Anthropic integration professionals. We're not a recruitment agency. We're a curated bench of verified AI specialists who deliver enterprise Claude deployments end-to-end. Think Big 4 consulting but focused entirely on the Anthropic ecosystem.

We're looking for engineers who want to be at the centre of this shift — building Claude API integrations, MCP servers, Cowork skills, and AI-first workflows for enterprise clients across financial services, government, healthcare, and professional services.

What you'll do on client engagements
Build production Claude API integrations connecting enterprise systems to AI-powered workflows
Design and deploy MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that give Claude secure access to internal tools and data
Create custom Cowork skills and plugins tailored to each client's departments and processes
Migrate legacy automation (RPA, scripted workflows, manual processes) to Claude-powered alternatives
Work alongside Solutions Architects, Change Managers, and Project Managers as part of a Galileo delivery team
What you bring, preferred experience 
3+ years software development experience in Python, TypeScript, or both
Strong API integration skills — you've connected systems, built middleware, or worked with REST/GraphQL APIs
Comfort working in enterprise environments with security requirements, stakeholder management, and documentation expectations
Genuine interest in AI and LLMs — you don't need to be an expert yet, but you need to be curious and fast-learning
Nice to have (not required)
Prior experience with Claude API, OpenAI API, or other LLM platforms
Familiarity with MCP, Cowork, or Claude Code
Experience with enterprise platforms (SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow) as integration targets
Cloud infrastructure experience (AWS, Azure, GCP)
What Galileo offers
Great contract rates depending on experience
Steady pipeline of enterprise clients — we handle sales, scoping, and contracts so you focus on delivery
Free Anthropic certification training — we'll get you certified if you aren't already
You don't need to be an Anthropic expert today. If you're a strong engineer who's excited about what AI is doing to enterprise software and you want to specialise in the ecosystem that's winning — we'll invest in getting you there. We provide the training, the certification pathway, and the client pipeline. You bring the engineering skills and the drive.

To apply: Submit your CV and a brief note on why the Anthropic ecosystem interests you. If you've built anything with Claude, LLMs, or AI tools — share it. We review every application personally.

Emesent is a world leader in autonomous drone technology, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Our solutions are transforming how mining, engineering, infrastructure, and public safety industries capture, understand, and act on spatial data.

Emesent is seeking a practical, hands-on Mechatronics Engineer to join our Hardware team. This role is focused on supporting the transition of products from development into production, improving the manufacturability and reliability of our hardware, and helping drive continuous improvement across the product lifecycle. 

You will work closely with hardware, embedded software, manufacturing, quality, and supply chain teams to introduce new products, resolve technical issues seen in production, and implement improvements that enhance product quality, robustness, and manufacturability.  

Key responsibilities

New Product Introduction 

Support the introduction of new products into manufacturing, ensuring designs, processes, tooling, and documentation are ready for production.

Work with design and manufacturing teams to improve product manufacturability, testability, serviceability, and production readiness.

Coordinate and support pilot builds, verification builds, and production ramp-up activities. 

Product Reliability & Quality Improvement 

Support product reliability improvement through structured investigation of field returns, test failures, and recurring production defects. 

Contribute to failure analysis, reliability testing, and validation of design or process changes. 

Help define and implement improvements that reduce product risk and improve long-term robustness in the field. 

Collaborate with hardware and embedded teams to resolve issues at the system level and verify the effectiveness of fixes. 

Continuous Improvement 

Monitor product and production performance data to identify trends, recurring issues, and opportunities for improvement. 

Lead or support root cause investigations and implement corrective and preventive actions. 

Improve manufacturing processes, test methods, and product designs to increase quality, reliability, efficiency, and consistency. 

Support operator training and process standardisation for new and existing products. 

Work cross-functionally to communicate engineering changes, process updates, and improvement initiatives. 

What you’ll bring

Degree in Mechatronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related discipline. 

Experience working with electromechanical products in a product development, manufacturing, NPI, or production engineering environment. 

Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills across mechanical, electrical, and embedded systems. 

Experience supporting manufacturing, production builds, or new product introduction activities. 

Experience creating technical documentation such as work instructions, test procedures, and process documentation. 

Strong communication skills and the ability to work effectively across engineering, manufacturing, quality, and operations teams. 

A hands-on approach and willingness to work closely with physical hardware, assemblies, tooling, and test systems. 

The added perks

9-day fortnight - every second Friday off

Employee Share Option Plan (ESOP) share in Emesent’s success

Work alongside a friendly and passionate team building cutting-edge technology

Genuine learning and development opportunities, including access to LinkedIn Learning

Employee Assistance Program

One full day of leave each year for your work anniversary

Novated Lease options

Who are we?

Emesent is a world leader in drone autonomy, LiDAR mapping, and spatial data analytics. Founded in 2018 with deep roots in Australia’s globally recognised CSIRO, we’ve built a reputation for delivering high-quality spatial data capture across mining, infrastructure, survey, and construction industries. Our flagship product, Hovermap, is an autonomous mobile scanning platform that combines advanced collision avoidance and autonomous flight with LiDAR mapping to capture data in hazardous, GPS-denied, and hard-to-reach environments. Mountable on drones, vehicles, or carried as a backpack, Hovermap has set the standard for mobile mapping in underground mining and confined spaces. Our newest product, GX1, extends Emesent’s mapping technology into the AEC and survey markets. GX1 is a ground-based LiDAR scanner purpose-built for above-ground
environments delivering fast, accurate spatial capture for surveying and reality capture professionals.

With a rapidly growing global customer base and multiple product lines spanning hardware, desktop software, and cloud, Emesent is at an exciting inflection point  and we’re looking for people who want to help shape what comes next.


Why Emesent?


At Emesent, you’ll work on technology that is genuinely shaping the future of how industries capture and understand the physical world. We offer a dynamic, collaborative environment, competitive compensation, and real opportunities for professional growth. Join us in pushing the boundaries of robotics and spatial intelligence.

We’re hiring a hands-on engineer with 4+ years’ experience to help us design, prototype, assemble, and validate innovative systems. If you enjoy moving from CAD or schematics to the bench, building proof‑of‑concepts, and instrumenting tests to make data‑driven decisions, you’ll feel right at home at Resonate Systems.

What you’ll do

Design & development of mechanical, mechatronic or electrical solutions from concept through to validated prototypes and small-scale builds.

Prototyping & assembly: build fixtures, rigs, looms, and sub‑assemblies; iterate quickly; support low‑volume assembly.

Test & measurement: plan and execute tests, instrument systems (sensors/DAQ), capture data, and analyse results to inform design decisions.

Documentation: specs, test plans, assembly instructions, risk assessments, and compliance/traceability.

Collaboration: work closely with cross‑functional engineers and suppliers; contribute to design reviews and continuous improvement.

You’ll bring

Degree in Mechanical, Mechatronic, or Electrical Engineering (or equivalent).

4+ years in product development / R&D / test & validation with tangible hardware outcomes.

Hands‑on prototyping skills (e.g., lab tools, soldering/wiring, 3D printing, basic machining/fasteners, safe workshop practices).

Instrumentation & test: familiarity with sensors, DAQ, oscilloscopes, multimeters, signal conditioning, and test planning.

Tools (one or more relevant):

Mech: CAD (e.g., SolidWorks, Inventor, Fusion 360), DFM/DFA, tolerancing, materials.

Elec: Schematic/PCB (e.g., Altium, KiCad), power & signal basics, wiring looms, EMC considerations.

Control/Test: MATLAB/Simulink, Python, or LabVIEW for data acquisition/automation.

Clear communication, structured problem solving, and ownership from concept to results.

Full working rights in Australia.

Nice to have

NI/LabVIEW & NI DAQ, PLCs or embedded (MCUs), Python test automation.

Systems engineering, requirements management, FMEA, risk/safety and relevant AS/ISO standards.

Experience with small‑scale assembly/pilot builds and supplier engagement.

Why Resonate Systems

Impactful work spanning design → build → test in a collaborative, hands‑on environment.

Opportunity to broaden your stack across mechanical, electrical, and test automation.

Support for professional development and continuous learning.

Location & Type

Adelaide, SA | [Onsite/Hybrid—customise] | Full‑time.

How to apply

Click Apply with your CV and a short cover letter outlining:

a project where you designed and prototyped hardware, and

how you instrumented/validated it.
Links to a portfolio, GitHub, or examples are welcome.

Resonate Systems is an equal opportunity employer. We value diverse perspectives and encourage applications from all backgrounds.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Ready to elevate your engineering career beyond the ordinary?

This isn't just another PLC engineering job - this is your chance to transform industries and impact lives through cutting-edge science and technology. Design world-class control systems that power Australia's most advanced research - enabling discoveries in medicine, clean energy, biotechnology, and beyond.


Why Join This Role?
Purpose-Driven Projects: Contribute to breakthroughs in critical sectors - health, food, energy, mining, nanotech, agriculture, and more
Collaboration at Its Best: Work alongside world-leading researchers, scientists, and engineers in a highly innovative, cross-disciplinary environment
Growth Without Limits: Expand your technical expertise while influencing national and global research outcomes
What You'll Do

Industrial robots: KUKA, Stäubli, Epson

The Operations & AI role is a strategic, senior role responsible for transforming data into intelligence that drives operational excellence .

This role goes beyond traditional reporting: the Analytics Specialist will harness the power of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and advanced analytics to optimise critical services delivery, improve driver and network efficiency, strengthen customer reporting, and support evidence-based decision-making at every level of the business.

Working at the intersection of data science, this leader will build scalable analytics capabilities that directly enhance patient outcomes, reduce operational risk, and support ambitious growth across Australia.

Key Responsibilities:

Analytics & Reporting

Collect, analyse, interpret, and present data from across the business to enable high-quality operational and executive decision-making

Use the reporting tools to create visually immersive, interactive, and real-time dashboards

Prepare management reports that clearly articulate trends, patterns, and forward-looking predictions using relevant operational data

Tie together disparate and unrelated data sets – including driver GPS data, POD records, booking system data, temperature logs, and financial data – to produce unified, actionable insights

Perform continuous quality assurance on all data pipelines and reporting, detecting breaks, anomalies, and integrity issues and determining solutions

AI & Advanced Analytics

Design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning models to optimise operations

Build and refine AI-generated routing and dispatch logic that accounts for live traffic conditions, driver availability, and urgency classification to reduce delivery times and improve service reliability

Develop predictive analytics models

Leverage AI to identify operational risk patterns

Evaluate and implement AI tools, large language models (LLMs), and automation capabilities that enhance workflow efficiency

Champion the use of generative AI for internal productivity – including prompt engineering frameworks, AI-assisted report narration, and intelligent alerting systems

Operational Strategy & Stakeholder Engagement

Work alongside cross functional teams to establish current business intelligence needs and prioritise the analytics roadmap accordingly

Lead cross-state workforce planning initiatives, influencing and governing cross-functional leaders and teams through data-driven insights rather than direct authority

Translate complex analytical findings into clear, practical recommendations that allow operational stakeholders to take immediate, realistic action

Facilitate collaborative planning sessions with stakeholders, aligning subject matter expertise with organisational strategy and data capability

Build trust and credibility with cross-functional stakeholders by embedding data and AI tools into everyday decision-making workflows

Drive accountability and execution by guiding teams from insight to action, ensuring all analytics initiatives are understood, owned, and delivered 

Data Infrastructure & Governance

Make recommendations on data collection methods, system integrations, and analytics infrastructure to improve data quality, coverage, and analytical efficiency

Collaborate with IT to ensure data from all of our systems

Academic Qualifications

Tertiary qualifications, preferably in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Technology, Statistics, or a related analytical discipline, with a high level of academic achievement

Postgraduate study, certifications, or specialised training in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or Applied Data Science is highly regarded

Professional certifications in AI/ML platforms (e.g. Microsoft Azure AI, Google Cloud AI, AWS Machine Learning) or data visualisation tools are advantageous

Principal Consulting AI / Data Engineer

DyFlex Solutions is an established SAP Platinum Partner with offices across Australia and New Zealand. We are recognised as the local leader in SAP expertise, awarded many times as the best performing partner in multiple categories in the last few years. We deliver fit-for-purpose SAP solutions, with services delivering greenfield, upgrade and transformative implementations. We also deliver the project management, change management and ongoing support services to enable our staff to deliver excellence for our customers.

As the leader in SAP, Dyflex is now establishing a team to become the leader in enabling business centric AI & Data solutions. With SAP integrating Datasphere as an extension to Data Lakes such as Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery and Microsoft Fabric, we are seeking pragmatic thought leaders to help shape the shift of AI & Data engineering into the era of agentic/context engineering.

About the Role:
As a Principal Consulting AI / Data Engineer, you will design, build, and optimise enterprise-scale data and AI solutions, while also leading engagements with executive stakeholders. This role requires both technical depth and consulting acumen: you will be expected to explain complex technical concepts clearly to non-technical directors, C-suite executives, and senior customer stakeholders. You’ll play a critical role in shaping DyFlex’s growing Data and AI practice, delivering innovative, business-aligned solutions that drive measurable outcomes.

Your tasks and responsibilities:

Design, build, and maintain scalable data and AI solutions using Databricks, cloud platforms, and modern frameworks
Lead solution architecture discussions with clients, ensuring alignment of technical delivery with business strategy
Present to and influence executive-level stakeholders, including boards, C-suite, and senior directors
Translate highly technical solutions into clear business value propositions for non-technical audiences
Mentor and guide teams of engineers and consultants to deliver high-quality solutions
Champion best practices across data engineering, MLOps, and cloud delivery
Build DyFlex’s reputation as a trusted partner in Data & AI through thought leadership and client advocacy

Your qualifications and experience:

Proven expertise in delivering enterprise-grade data engineering and AI solutions in production environments for corporate, enterprise or public sector organisations
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL, plus experience with Spark, Airflow, dbt, Kafka, or Flink
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and AI/Data platforms including Databricks, Snowflake, Google BigQuery, Microsoft Fabric
Ability to confidently communicate and present at C-suite level, simplifying technical concepts into business impact
Track record of engaging senior executives and influencing strategic decisions
Strong consulting and stakeholder management skills with client-facing experience
Experience with agentic coding tools such as Claude Code, Cortex in personal projects or commercial context, highly regarded
Background in MLOps, ML pipelines, or AI solution delivery highly regarded
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related field

What we offer:

Work with SAP’s latest technologies on cloud as S/4HANA, BTP and Joule, plus Databricks, ML/AI tools and cloud platforms
A flexible and supportive work environment including work from home
Competitive remuneration and benefits including novated lease, birthday leave, salary packaging, wellbeing programme, additional purchased leave, and company-provided laptop
Comprehensive training budget and paid certifications (Databricks, SAP, cloud platforms)
Structured career advancement pathways with opportunities to lead large-scale client programs
Exposure to diverse industries and client environments, including executive-level engagement
Join a renowned organisation delivering projects to some of Australia’s leading enterprises

DyFlex is committed to providing a safe, flexible and respectful environment for staff free from all forms of discrimination, bullying and harassment. We are proud of our diverse and inclusive team as only together we can continually improve ourselves and achieve best outcomes for our customers– we are the region's leading SAP Platinum Partner!

Please note that we can’t offer sponsorship for this role and we expect a clear statement about your legal rights in Australia, especially if you are applying from overseas.

Who We Are

We are an Australian-owned road transport equipment engineer and manufacturer head-quartered in South Australia and operating nationally, with a proven track record of product delivery, performance, and customer service. We provide technical services to Australia's heavy vehicle organisations that are associated with mining, quarrying, construction and material handling.

About the role

We are seeking a commercially minded AI strategist to lead our company’s digital transformation initiatives. This role is responsible for identifying opportunities for optimisation and automation, and driving the successful implementation of AI driven solutions across the business. You will work closely with the executive team, department heads, and IT specialists to ensure the AI initiatives align with business goals and deliver measurable value. 

Key Responsibilities

Develop and execute a practical AI and digital transformation roadmap aligned with business priorities

Identify and implement AI solutions across key areas including:

Workshop scheduling and workflow optimisation

Predictive maintenance and equipment efficiency

Quality control and defect reduction

Administrative automation

Enhancing data visibility and reporting for better decision making

Design and deploy internal AI tools, such as process assistants and knowledge sharing platforms for staff

Work with key departments to streamline processes and reduce manual workload

Lead end to end implementation of AI initiatives, from opportunity identification through to roll out and adoption

Drive change management to ensure uptake across the business

Establish governance frameworks to ensure ethical, compliant, and secure use of AI

About You

We’re looking for someone who has:

Proven experience delivering AI, automation, or digital transformation initiatives in a practical business environment

A strong background in AI agent development and AI driven automation

The ability to translate business problems into simple, scalable technology solutions

Experience working with both blue collar and white collar environments

Focused on outcomes, efficiency and continuous improvement

Reporting to the Executive Team, this role will be a very 'hands on' role that gives you the opportunity to work as a key member of a growing and entrepreneurial organisation.

If you have a passion for delivering business outcomes, apply online.

Salary: Generous salary, pay reviews & retail discounts
Ad Text:

We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

As a Hardware Engineering Graduate at Lockheed Martin, you will be applying your knowledge and experience using various technologies, tools and processes to develop effective approved solutions. Research, design, document, prototype and investigate systems

Coding and unit testing 

Design and develop components for installation

Correct errors in existing maintained systems

Equal Opportunity Statement

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees’ unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:



Senior DevOps Engineer - AI | Technology & GenAI - Productionisation & Automation | Melbourne

Be at the forefront of our AI journey

Choice and flexibility on where, when and how you work

Learn from the best in the business 

What will your typical day look like?

As a Senior Systems Engineer, you'll be at the heart of Deloitte's ClearTrack Generative AI & Digital Assets platform, helping design, build and run highly secure, scalable GenAI infrastructure that powers real-world outcomes. You'll work closely with AI engineers, developers and architects to keep platforms fast, resilient and production-ready — while continuously evolving our stack to stay ahead of emerging AI technologies.

In this role, you will:

Design, implement and operate the central GenAI & Assets platform, built on Kubernetes

Engineer reliable, secure cloud infrastructure across AWS and/or Azure

Monitor performance, availability and cost, optimising systems for scale and speed

Troubleshoot complex system, software and networking issues

Own system security, patching, updates and resilience

Drive Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD and DevSecOps best practice

Ensure robust backup, disaster recovery and platform documentation

Partner with IT, security and engineering teams to integrate new AI capabilities

About the team

The National Generative AI and Assets team sits within Deloitte's Strategy, Transformation & Innovation office — a group shaping the future of how the firm delivers value to its largest and most complex clients.

We bring together Design, AI and Software Engineering to build enterprise-grade GenAI platforms and digital products that improve productivity, decision-making and business performance. Our work moves quickly from idea to production, combining cutting-edge technology with strong governance and security.

If you're excited by scaling AI responsibly in enterprise environments, this is where it happens.

What success looks like:

High availability, low-latency GenAI platforms running in production
Secure, compliant systems with minimal downtime
Well-documented, automated infrastructure and deployment pipelines
Continuous improvements to performance, reliability and developer experience
On-time delivery of platform enhancements and upgrades

Enough about us — let's talk about you

You bring strong systems engineering fundamentals plus hands-on GenAI platform experience.

You'll likely have:

5+ years' experience as a Systems Engineer (GenAI exposure highly valued)

A degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics or similar (preferred)

Experience supporting platforms in mature, managed services environments

Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate across diverse teams

Technical experience we're looking for:

Core engineering

AWS and/or Azure cloud platforms

Linux/UNIX systems administration

Networking, databases, messaging and security fundamentals

Infrastructure as Code (Terraform)

CI/CD and DevSecOps tools (GitHub, ADO, ArgoCD)

Identity and security protocols (OAuth, SAML, OpenID Connect)

Generative AI & platform experience:

Designing, building and operating AI chatbots and conversational AI systems

Hands-on experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, Haystack, OpenAI / Azure OpenAI)

Building RAG-based solutions (document ingestion, embeddings, semantic search)

Experience with agentic and automation frameworks (n8n, LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI)

Strong understanding of AI tooling: vector databases, prompt management, model evaluation

Integrating AI systems with enterprise platforms, APIs and workflows

Solid software engineering skills (Python and/or JavaScript) in production environments

Knowledge of AI governance, security and responsible AI in enterprise settings

Why Deloitte?  

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.   
We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.  
We support flexibility and choice. We encourage you to find the right balance between connecting in person with your clients and teams and meeting your own personal needs.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.  

Next Steps

Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

Amentum is continually expanding our opportunities working on prominent defence projects across air, land and sea supporting the critical capabilities that keep Australia safe. We have a growing presence in cyber, space and nuclear programs.
  
Tell me about this role:
Working within a multidisciplinary team, you’ll play a key role in maintaining engineering governance, developing technical documentation, and supporting system verification and validation activities across the project lifecycle.
  
As a Level 2 Systems Engineer, you’ll provide clarity and structure to engineering decisions, support rigorous review of technical data, and collaborate closely with stakeholders to maintain accurate architecture and configuration documentation. You’ll help ensure system maturity, requirements traceability, and compliance with Defence standards, while contributing to risk management, system security considerations, and emerging technical challenges.


What’s involved?

Support the maintenance and currency of project engineering plans and processes, including the Engineering Management Plan (EMP) and Test and Evaluation Master Plan (TEMP).
Develop and coordinate engineering reports in accordance with project standards and direction.
Support the engineering team to conduct reviews of technical data, including CDRLs, plans, Technical Instructions, RFIs, CCPs, and ECPs.
Provide engineering input on current and emerging project risks to support project risk management activities.
Support system integration and verification and validation (V&V) activities.
Provide engineering input on design maturity and compliance against project requirements and standards.
Support the engineering team in addressing system security and Reliability, Maintainability and Availability (RMA) requirements.
  
What sort of skills do I need:

Bachelor's degree in engineering or similar qualification
Strong systems engineering capability across requirements, V&V, integration and engineering governance.
Ability to analyse and review technical data (CDRLs, RFIs, ECPs, furnished data) and support informed engineering decisions.
Proficiency in developing and maintaining engineering plans, architecture documentation, and configuration management
Skilled in stakeholder engagement, risk input, and supporting system maturity, compliance, and RMA considerations across complex projects.
Australian Citizenship
Active NV1 Clearance
ITAR Compliant
  
What’s in it for me?
Flexibility - We like our employees to have the flexibility they need to live the lives they choose. With flexible working options such as WFH where possible.
Inclusion - Our employees should feel safe bringing their whole self to work, we offer a number of programs promoting inclusion.
Benefits and Rewards – Our benefits and rewards include additional superannuation; flexible leave options; salary continuation insurance; professional certification bonus; employee discount program.
   
Amentum: Trusted Partner in National Security

Amentum is a key service provider to the Australian Government, supporting critical national security and Defence capabilities across land, air, maritime, cyber, space, nuclear, and digital domains.
  
With over 880 professionals nationwide, we deliver complex infrastructure, logistics, and technical solutions with a strong focus on quality, safety, and innovation.

With 27+ years of experience and a presence in major cities and regions, we’re proud of our people—our greatest asset. Join us and be part of a high-performing team with exceptional benefits and career opportunities. Apply directly or reach out to Symmone Chapman, symmone.chapman@global.amentum.com

Welcome to MVSI!

We are a dynamic team of 70+ professionals, spread across the Globe with offices in Australia, EMEA, the US and the Philippines. Our innovative SaaS product is trusted by banks, Fintech’s, and crypto services worldwide to manage their AML/KYC Compliance requirements. We have always harnessed the power of cutting-edge technology and are currently exploring the exciting realm of LLM (think ChatGPT) to further elevate our offerings. Our company culture is built on a foundation of support and commitment to career development. 

We truly value our people and invite you to join us to experience all that we have to offer!

Why Apply?

Get an awesome career! Ours is a specialised industry, and experience is in short supply. We employ people from all walks of life and train them to be certified professionals over time. This is a fabulous opportunity to find a new direction, build transferable skills, and enjoy a profession where every day is interesting, and you'll want to come to work.

Job Summary

Our Stack: Material UI, NodeJS, React, MongoDB.

Design, build, and maintain well-architected, production-grade systems aligned with MVSI engineering standards

Develop and maintain scalable MERN-based services with a strong focus on quality, performance, and test coverage

Improve system reliability, security, resilience, and cost efficiency across the platform

Decompose and modernise legacy or monolithic systems into scalable microservices

Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business requirements into robust technical solutions

Participate in shared operational support and contribute to production stability and incident resolution

Champion continuous improvement, engineering best practices, and automation

Learn, grow, and help raise the engineering bar across the team

Technical Skills

5+ years of commercial software development experience

5+ years of TypeScript / JavaScript development

Extensive experience with the MERN stack:

MongoDB (or other NoSQL databases, or Postgresql etc)

Node.js

React.js

Jest or equivalent testing frameworks

Proven experience designing and building RESTful APIs with strong security and integration practices

Demonstrated ability to decompose monoliths into microservices

Strong understanding of secure coding practices and application security

A passion for clean, maintainable, testable code and collaborative development

Experience using AI-assisted development tools to improve productivity, quality, and innovation

Desirable (Nice to Have)

Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) experience, including:

-          Reliability engineering practices

-          Incident management and post-incident reviews

-          SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets

Experience operating high-availability, customer-facing SaaS platforms·Exposure to performance engineering and cost optimisation in AWS


Personal Skills:

High ownership mindset with strong accountability and initiative

Growth-oriented, adaptable, and open to feedback

Customer-focused, with a clear understanding of business and user impact

Confident communicator who collaborates effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders

Strong analytical thinking with a bias for action and outcomes

Willingness to mentor others and share knowledge

Organised, pragmatic, and proactive in improving team productivity and engineering standards




Why Join MVSI

Be part of a mission-driven team simplifying compliance and risk management for global businesses.

Work on a modern SaaS product with real impact in fintech/regtech.

Flexible remote or hybrid work options.

A culture that values curiosity, quality, and teamwork.

Competitive salary, equity options, and professional growth opportunities

And the little things - Drinks, snacks, flex time, birthdays off, and much much more.

Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

About the role:

The Platform Security Engineer is a valued member of our engineering team. We value evidence-backed opinions and offer the opportunity to play an integral part in shaping engineering rhythm, culture, and processes across planning, architecture and design, coding, deployment, and support.


In an ever-changing environment, Lendi Group is leading the charge in developing industry-first technology designed to offer Australian homeowners transparency, simplicity, and convenience in their home loan experience.
The Platform Security Engineer partners with engineering teams to evaluate planned changes, minimise security risk, and continuously improve our security posture. A key focus of the role is driving vulnerability and security findings down over time, improving security standards, and embedding secure-by-default practices across both traditional and AI-enabled systems.

Please note: this role will be a 12 Month Fixed Term Contract

Job Description


Working closely with the Cyber Security team to ensure security principles are consistently applied across the engineering environment.
Collaborating and coding with engineering teams to uplift secure development practices and reduce recurring security findings.
Designing, building, and maintaining secure cloud and microservice solutions.
Improving security posture through effective use of COTS products, platform controls, and network infrastructure.
Vulnerability and Threat Management — monitoring and assessing assets including cloud infrastructure, containers, packages, APIs, and requests, with a focus on remediation and measurable risk reduction.
Supporting the secure design and operation of AI-enabled systems, including LLM integrations, automation agents, and MCP-based architectures.
Implementing and reviewing AI security guardrails such as access controls, data protection, prompt-injection mitigation, and output validation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams including engineering, operations, compliance, data, and AI platform teams.
Contributing as an individual contributor within a cross-functional DevOps / SecOps team.
Build or integrate services to improve security and compliance

Qualifications


Strong understanding of networking, infrastructure, and applications from a DevOps perspective with a security focus.
Hands-on experience implementing and maintaining security controls across cloud environments (particularly AWS) and access management solutions (e.g. Auth0).
Experience with security logging, monitoring, and vulnerability assessment, with a demonstrated ability to drive findings down and improve baseline security standards.
Passion for secure coding practices and identifying common implementation and operational security risks.

Desirable Experience

AWS technologies such as CloudFormation, Terraform, IAM, WAF, Node.js and/or TypeScript.
Docker, containerised environments, serverless architectures, and Kubernetes.
Event-driven systems.
Securing AI platforms, including LLM integrations, MCP implementations, AI guardrails, and automation/build agents.

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
An additional week’s Loyalty Leave each year after reaching 3 years’ service.
Wellness initiatives with a strong focus on psychological safety.

We’re committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive community at Lendi Group. We believe that a team reflecting the world around us leads to greater innovation, stronger collaboration, and a more engaging workplace.

Our culture is guided by our 3 core values: We Are Stronger United; Act Like You Own It; and Keep Home Loans Human. Our values are part of our core DNA that helps Lendi Group to attract, engage and evolve the right talent and build best-in-class products.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing, purpose-driven company that’s transforming the homeownership journey.

Ready to contribute to Lendi Group’s next chapter? Apply now and be part of something big!

Ad Text:
Overview:

Are you keen to design and develop world-class capabilities, turning data into knowledge and operational outcomes? Are you passionate about building solutions to allow data to tell its story? ASIS offers an opportunity to achieve these goals in an uncompromisingly unique environment. As a Data Professional in ASIS, you will have the chance to actively enable our operational teams to make the best use of ASIS’s unique data assets.

ASIS leverages the wide world of open-source software and supplements it with our own development to deliver the bespoke tools required for the ASIS mission. Among our core technologies are Python, Scala, Java, TypeScript, and JavaScript. Our stack includes Spring, Vue, PyTorch, Tensorflow, Apache Spark, Accumulo, Hadoop, NiFi, Solr, Ansible and Git. We leverage structured and unstructured data, including geospatial and multilingual datasets. Ideal candidates will be extraordinarily innovative, self-motivated and keen to use these toolsets to craft new systems that are simply not found in any other organisation.

If this sounds like an environment you could thrive in, then apply for one of these unique positions.

Specialisations

We have a number of positions available including:

Software Engineers at L4 to EL1
Data Engineers at L 6 to EL1
Data Technicians at L4 to Level 6
System Engineers at L4 to EL1
DevOps Engineers at L4 to EL1
Data Centre Facilities Manager at EL1 to EL2 (Canberra only)
For more detail pleas visit the ASIS website (Current Vacancies) and review the selection document under the Talent Register - Data Engineering Professionals L4 - EL2

Job Title: Software Engineering Manager, PDEIO Test Results
Ad Text:
At Google, we have a vision of empowerment and equitable opportunity for all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and commit to building reconciliation through Google’s technology, platforms and people and we welcome Indigenous applicants. Please see our Reconciliation Action Plan for more information.

Minimum qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree, or equivalent practical experience.
8 years of experience in software development.
3 years of experience with full-stack development, with back-end such as Java, Python, Golang, or C++ codebases, and front-end including JavaScript or TypeScript, HTML, CSS.
3 years of experience in a technical leadership role.
2 years of experience in a people management or team leadership role.
Preferred qualifications:

Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science or a related technical field.
5 years of experience with leading teams in testing, maintaining, or launching software products, and 2 years of experience with leading Machine Learning (ML) design or landing ML-powered features or products.
3 years of experience with leading teams in building developer tools (e.g., compilers, automated releases, code design and testing, test automation frameworks).
Experience in working on open source projects and with open source ecosystems.
Experience in using data science or statistical methods to develop insights and solve problems.
About the job


Like Google's own ambitions, the work of a Software Engineer goes beyond just Search. Software Engineering Managers have not only the technical expertise to take on and provide technical leadership to major projects, but also manage a team of Engineers. You not only optimize your own code but make sure Engineers are able to optimize theirs. As a Software Engineering Manager you manage your project goals, contribute to product strategy and help develop your team. Teams work all across the company, in areas such as information retrieval, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking, security, data compression, user interface design; the list goes on and is growing every day. Operating with scale and speed, our exceptional software engineers are just getting started -- and as a manager, you guide the way.

With technical and leadership expertise, you manage engineers across multiple teams and locations, a large product budget and oversee the deployment of large-scale projects across multiple sites internationally.


The Platform and Devices Engineering Infrastructure and Operations (PDEIO) team is focused on developing test surfaces to dramatically increase the productivity of development teams working on Android, Chromium, and ChromeOS. The larger team owns the end-to-end development lifecycle, including code generation, planning, reviews, testing, and triaging. We collaborate with Google's research and other internal teams to create industry leading solutions for our customer's specific needs (Chrome, ChromeOS, and Android developers).

The Google Pixel team focuses on designing and delivering the world's most helpful mobile experience. The team works on shaping the future of Pixel devices and services through some of the most advanced designs, techniques, products, and experiences in consumer electronics. This includes bringing together the best of Google’s artificial intelligence, software, and hardware to build global smartphones and create transformative experiences for users across the world.

Responsibilities

Recruit, build, motivate and inspire a team to build agents into development workflows and platforms.
Guide team in designing, building, and maintaining back-end services, Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) and User Interface (UI) to support the agents.
Work with data pipelines to collect, process, and analyze data for model evaluation and metric tracking.
Navigate ambiguous and complex problem spaces.

Work with evolving and changing technology.
Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

Category:
Consultants (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
What you'll do  

Manage the design, planning and implementation of technical solutions, ensuring alignment with best practice IM/ICT design and delivery frameworks.
Specifies and designs complex systems and recommends appropriate design standards, methods and tools, consistent with agreed enterprise and solution architectures and ensures effective application.
Maintains an in-depth knowledge of technical specialisms and provides expert advice regarding Identity and Access application.
Automating User Lifecycle Processes: Implementing solutions for user provisioning, modification, and deprovisioning to streamline identity workflows 
Ensuring Compliance: Maintaining alignment with organisational policies, security standards, and audit requirements 
Enterprise Integration: Designing and executing integrations between IAM systems and enterprise applications to support seamless access and data flow 
System Customization & Maintenance: Tailoring NetIQ IAM components to meet evolving business needs and ensuring ongoing system stability and performance 
This position demands a strong understanding of identity governance, access control, and enterprise architecture, along with the ability to collaborate across technical and business teams to deliver secure, scalable solutions. 
Manage the development, publication, implementation and compliance of all program/project(s) deliverables and related standards and procedures at an enterprise level.

About you  

We are searching for someone who has:

Experience in the design, publication, implementation, compliance and delivery of technical solutions in enterprise systems to industry best practice frameworks for large scale, complex IM/ICT program/project(s) with experience in some or all of the following hardware software: NetIQ Identity Manager driver development, Virtual Infrastructure, Windows, Unix and Linux Server Operating Environments, Cloud and Directory Services – Entra/AzureAD, eDirectory and ADFS, ServiceNow integration and PowerShell Scripting, preferably in the healthcare industry.  (SFIA Skill: ITMG: Level 5, HSIN: Level 4)
Experience designing systems and providing expert knowledge in relevant design standards, techniques and methods and tools for large scale complex organisations. (SFIA Skill: DESN: Level 4, METL: 5)
Demonstrated experience in the assessment, analysis, development, documentation and implementation planning of changes for large complex IM/ICT program/project(s). (SFIA Skill: CHMG: Level 4)

Why work with us?  

Work alongside passionate professionals in a supportive and inclusive environment that values people and prioritises employee success and wellbeing. 

Competitive salaries 

12.75% superannuation  
17.5% leave loading 

Employee wellbeing & development 

Access to 24/7 confidential employee support providers and counsellors including for immediate family members 
Additional flexibility to support your work life balance including access to generous leave entitlements, such as purchased leave, domestic violence leave, reproductive health leave, cultural leave, study and examination leave. 
Access to a variety of programs and initiatives to support training and career growth.  
Grow your skills through hands-on experience and access to internal training opportunities and additional financial and leave benefits for approved external training and development. 

Our commitment to equity, diversity and inclusion  

At Queensland Health, our work environment is inclusive and supportive, and we value our employees. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage applications from people of all cultures, abilities and backgrounds.  

Our commitment to cultural safety, equity, diversity and inclusion means we understand some people may need changes to the recruitment process. If you need support during the recruitment process, such as meeting with the panel virtually instead of in person, please reach out to the hiring manager. We value diverse candidates and your need for adjustments will not affect our hiring decisions.  

Ready to apply? 

For further information on how to apply please review the attached Role Description.
